title: Document operator-facing impact of stack-agnostic guardrail updates
description: Capture the user-facing documentation impact for this spec, explicitly confirming whether any additional docs changes are needed beyond the template edits and recording the memory-first test-command behavior in the bead handoff.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation output states that the change is limited to framework templates unless an additional doc update is required during implementation.
The handoff clearly explains the new memory-first test-command guidance for future operators and agents.
No unnecessary documentation churn is introduced outside the spec scope.

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed all three changed template files against the spec acceptance criteria and git diff. All changes are in place: test-execution/SKILL.md has the memory-first command selection flow replacing hardcoded Python commands; tester-validation/SKILL.md has stack-agnostic phrasing replacing unittest-specific wording; merge-conflict.md has memory-first + multi-stack detection replacing the stale codex_orchestrator/py_compile lines. No additional documentation files need updating — the templates are the operator/agent-facing documentation themselves.
handoff_summary.remaining: None. The spec scope was documentation-only (template edits). All acceptance criteria are met functionally. The only minor nuance is that uv run pytest appears in the detection hint table as an example of what a Python project likely uses — this is contextually correct and not a hardcoded prescription.
handoff_summary.risks: Low. docs/onboarding.md line 58 still references python -m py_compile as a default value for the user-configurable build/syntax check command in takt init prompts — this is out of spec scope (user-configurable default, not hardcoded agent instruction) but may be worth a future cleanup bead if non-Python onboarding is a priority.
handoff_summary.next_action: Review bead (B-3912d43e) can proceed — no documentation gaps, no blocking issues, all templates are consistent with spec intent.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. This bead made no file changes. The three template files are exclusively owned by this feature tree.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: The memory-first pattern for test command discovery is now the canonical approach in all tester-facing skills and guardrails. Agents detect once, write to shared memory (--namespace global), and all subsequent agents read from memory rather than re-detecting. This is a documentation-only change — no runtime behavior, no config changes, no new skills required.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No code changes were made. All existing tests pass (confirmed by prior tester bead in dependency chain).
handoff_summary.known_limitations: docs/onboarding.md line 58 still shows python -m py_compile as the default value for the build/syntax check command prompt in takt init — this is an onboarding UX default (user-overridable), not a hardcoded agent instruction, and is explicitly out of spec scope. A future standalone bead could update this default to something more generic.

conflict_risks: None. This bead made no file changes. The three template files are exclusively owned by this feature tree.

execution_history.summary: Confirmed all three template changes are complete and accurate; no additional documentation updates required beyond the template edits already landed.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before writing any documentation, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries to inform documentation; skip entries that don't apply.

Do **not** write to memory — documentation agents are read-only.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
